data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: YouTube employs a microservices architecture to manage the vast amount of video content and user interactions.
data.technical_spec: Containers like Kubernetes orchestrate different services that handle video processing, recommendation algorithms, and user management.

data.title: Scalable Storage Systems
data.description: Utilizes custom-designed storage solutions to manage exabytes of video data.
data.technical_spec: Google's Colossus distributed file system underlies the storage infrastructure.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Leverages a global CDN to ensure fast video delivery.
data.technical_spec: Servers are distributed globally to cache video content closer to the end-users.

data.title: Data Center Efficiency
data.description: Operates energy-efficient data centers for better resource utilization.
data.technical_spec: Custom hardware designed to optimize performance per watt.

data.title: Load Balancing and Traffic Management
data.description: Effective load balancing ensures an even distribution of network traffic.
data.technical_spec: Google Front End (GFE) manages HTTP(S) requests and distributes them to available backends.
